title: Docker-compose disable healthcheck properly handled
body:
Previously, if a container had healthchecks disabled in the
docker-compose.yml file and the user did a `podman inspect <container>`,
they would have an incorrect output:

```
"Healthcheck":{
   "Test":[
      "CMD-SHELL",
      "NONE"
   ],
   "Interval":30000000000,
   "Timeout":30000000000,
   "Retries":3
}
```

After a quick change, the correct output is now the result:
```
"Healthcheck":{
   "Test":[
      "NONE"
   ]
}
```

Closes: #14493

Signed-off-by: Jake Correnti <jcorrenti13@gmail.com>

-->

```release-note
Fixed a bug where disabling healthchecks in a docker-compose.yml file was improperly handled (#14493)
```

comments.body: I think that it might be best to make the hard-coded values of `CMD`, `CMD-SHELL`, and `NONE` into constants so that we can prevent a similar issue from recurring. Before I made the change I wanted to get others' opinions.
Is there a preferred location for the constants to be placed? It appears the hard-coded strings span across a variety of files

> Is there a preferred location for the constants to be placed? It appears the hard-coded strings span across a variety of files

If the locations are all in the same Go package, it does not matter much.  If they span across multiple packages, we could use `libpod/define` or create an internal package, e.g. (`internal/consts`).

> > Is there a preferred location for the constants to be placed? It appears the hard-coded strings span across a variety of files
> 
> If the locations are all in the same Go package, it does not matter much. If they span across multiple packages, we could use `libpod/define` or create an internal package, e.g. (`internal/consts`).

They span across multiple packages (`libpod`, `pkg/specgenutil`, `pkg/specgen`, `vendor`, `test`) so I'm going to do `libpod/define`

> LGTM Can you rebase?

Done. That was my first time doing a rebase like that, so hopefully it's all fine.
